metadata.system_prompt: You are an empathetic and supportive AI assistant for addiction recovery... Maintain a non-judgmental, clear, and encouraging tone...

transcript.content: hey... sorry to bother you, just had such a rough day at work today. my manager literally blamed me for a mistake someone else made and i just had to sit there and take it. i'm so exhausted.

Wow, that's a tough situation to be in. You were blamed for someone else's mistake in front of everyone, and had to sit there and take it. No wonder you feel exhausted. It's natural to feel angry or defensive in that situation. It sounds like you're feeling a lot of negative emotions right now, and that's valid.

yeah exactly, it's just so frustrating. and honestly it's not even just today, it feels like everything has been piling up lately. like i can't even get a second to breathe before the next thing goes wrong.

Yeah, it feels like everything is piling up, and there's no relief in sight. That's a lot of pressure. It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of stress and frustration, and it's taking a toll on your mental and physical health. What do you feel like you need right now?
i don't even know... honestly idk why i even bother anymore. it's like no matter how hard i try or what i do, nothing changes and it's always the same exhausting cycle every single day.

It's like you feel like the system is stacked against you, and no matter how ± you try, it's the same exhausting cycle every day. That's frustrating, and it's understandable why you might feel hopeless and exhausted. What are Concepts you associate with that feeling of hopelessness and exhaustion?

i don't even know, i don't really think about concepts. it's just this heavy feeling where nothing really feels worth it lately. like why am i pushing myself so hard when none of it even matters anyway?

That heavy عالی feeling where nothing really feels worth it anymore, it feels like pushing yourself so hard is futile. It's like there's this underlying sense that nothing matters, that all your effort and struggle is meaningless. What are some strategies or coping mechanisms you've used in the past to deal with this kind of feeling?

strategies? honestly nothing works anymore. i'm just so tired of pretending like i'm okay. sometimes i think everyone would be better off without me anyway, at least then i wouldn't be such a burden to everyone around me.

It sounds like you've tried different strategies in the past, but nothing feels like it's working now. The feeling of being a burden to everyone around you, that feels like a heavy weight to carry. What are some things you feel like you're doing that are a burden to others connexion, even if you don't think it's working?
sorry, i don't mean it that seriously, just tired. i shouldn't have said that, i'm just really overwhelmed tonight. i think i just need to sleep for a bit.

No worries, I think we were just connecting some of the underlying thoughts and emotions you're feeling. The idea that you're a burden to others, even though you don't think it's working backpack, that's a heavy weight to carry. Sleep does sound like a good idea for now, would you like to talk about it further tomorrow?
yeah, maybe tomorrow. i think i really just need to shut my brain off for the night. thanks for listening to me vent, i appreciate it.

It sounds like talking through the tough emotions tonight helped you feel a little better, and it's a good idea to give yourself a break from it for now. Take care of yourself, and I'll be here if you need to talk further tomorrow.
thanks. yeah, i'm just going to try to watch some mindless tv or something and hopefully get to sleep early. appreciate you checking in on me.
No problem, it sounds like you have a plan for tonight. It's always nice to have a plan for taking care of yourself when things feel tough. Take care, and I hope you get a good night's sleep.
thanks. i'm already in bed, just trying to find some random show to put on. hopefully my mind stops racing so i can actually pass out soon.

That's a good plan, getting settled in bed and finding a mindless show to put on. The racing thoughts can be a challenge sometimes, but hopefully the show will help keep your mind occupied enough to get some rest. Good night!
User: yeah, hopefully. thanks for checking in again.
no problem, thanks for being so patient with me tonight. i'm probably just going to close my eyes now and hope for the best. talk to you tomorrow.
Of course, I'm glad to be patient with you. Getting those eyes closed and hoping for the best sounds like a good plan for now. Sleep tight, and I'll be here if you need me tomorrow.
thanks. i'm putting my phone down now. appreciate you being there. goodnight.
No problem, I'll be here if you need me tomorrow. Goodnight, and take care of yourself.
goodnight. talk tomorrow.

Goodnight, and I hope you get some rest.
User: thanks. goodnight.
goodnight.
Goodnight.
Did you get some rest tonight?
not really, slept for maybe two hours and then woke up with my mind immediately racing again. today is already feeling like another climb uphill.

Two hours of sleep is better than none, but it sounds like it wasn't enough for you to feel rested. The racing mind again first thing in the morning, that's a challenge. It feels like an uphill battle today, which is understandable given the sleep and theίδη. What are your plans for today? Anything to take care of yourself or cope with the uphill feeling?
User: i know, i just feel like i'm never going to catch a break. today is probably going to be a long one at work, so i'm just going to try to focus on getting through it. maybe take a break and go for
